title: RSA migration: rewrite RsaPrivateKey and RsaPublicKey in Rust
description:
Rewrite wolfcrypt/src/rsa.rs to use the native wolfcrypt_rsa_* shims instead of EVP/OpenSSL compat functions. This is the main migration issue.

Scope:
- Replace all EVP_PKEY, RSA*, BIGNUM, i2d_*/d2i_* API calls with wolfcrypt_rsa_* shims
- RsaPrivateKey: holds opaque void* ctx; impl generate, from_pkcs1_der, to_pkcs1_der, to_public_spki_der, sign_pkcs1v15, sign_pss, decrypt_oaep, decrypt_pkcs1v15
- RsaPublicKey: holds opaque void* ctx; impl from_spki_der, to_spki_der, verify_pkcs1v15, verify_pss, encrypt_oaep, encrypt_pkcs1v15, from_private_key (export public from private)
- Remove wolfssl_openssl_extra from any RSA-specific cfg gates (the shims work without OPENSSL_EXTRA)
- All unsafe blocks confined to wolfcrypt-rs (no unsafe in wolfcrypt/src/rsa.rs)

Preserve exactly:
- Public API surface (all existing method names and signatures)
- Key format contract: private key = PKCS#1 DER, public key = SPKI DER

Do NOT change:
- Test structure (tests live in wolfcrypt-conformance, not in rsa.rs)
- Any non-RSA files

Acceptance criteria:
- cargo build -p wolfcrypt succeeds
- All existing RSA-related tests in wolfcrypt-conformance pass
- wolfssl_openssl_extra no longer required in RSA code paths
